author: codex

patch.value: Align Toon documentation with the emitted type and data envelope

The Toon guide promises a meta object containing timestamp, format, and version, and repeats it in 31 examples. The actual formatter emits only the stable type and compact data envelope. This false contract can break consumers that require nonexistent fields.
Overview and structure sections describe only type and data.; No example includes the nonexistent meta envelope.
The guide documents and demonstrates exactly the envelope emitted by formatToon.
The guide claims every response has metadata that the implementation does not emit.
Automation may reject valid CLI output or rely on timestamp/version fields that are always absent.

message: Track confirmed Toon documentation contract drift

value.author: codex
value.text: Removed the nonexistent meta contract from the guide overview,TypeScript interface,and all 31 repeated output examples.

author: codex

patch.value: Guide and formatter are aligned; repository search finds no stale meta envelope claims.
Documentation demonstrates only fields emitted by formatToon.
Removed all 31 nonexistent meta examples, corrected the overview and TypeScript interface, and retained the canonical type/data contract.
Toon guide now matches the emitted compact envelope.

message: Close verified Toon documentation contract issue
